title: altamiranoa jurgensenii
intro: Altamiranoa jurgensenii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the Mexican yellow rose, native to the tropical regions of Mexico and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of alkaloids, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Altamiranoa jurgensenii include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, among other uses. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing its leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
